b: ^ Boss Man is currently under suspicion.

Intro: ^That wasn't me, it was the other guy.

c-1: ^Yeah, real nice... Business is already bad as it is, a totaled car right behind the shop is going to do wonders for the customers.
c-2: ^Oh, the other guy. Of course. The one standing here talking to me right now about the other guy. THAT guy. Him.

c-0: ^Alright. Enough of this bit, it was obviously you.
c-1: ^Really? I never would've guessed. I could've sworn you said it was the other guy!

c-3: ^Go fucking where? Your car is impaled through that tree!

s0: ^So, what are we gonna do about it?

s1: ^ For crashing my car behind your shop epic style.

c-1: ^That wasn't so hard, now was it? Now get lost, I don't wanna see your face again.

b: ^Actually... I kinda need to take your statement. I'm here on behalf of Peter.

CanQuestion: ^What's going on, big guy? Crash any cars lately?

b: ^ I don't have time for this bozo, I've got a crime scene to investigate.

Police: ^The officer needs to take your statement. Again...
^Are you kidding me? What more can I say that I haven't already told you people? How incompetent are you!?
^You don't really have much of a choice. He's expecting you by the scene, so if I were you I would get going.
^Unbelievable... This is what my skattepengar goes to.
^Beautiful, he's left the shop unattended. Now I can have a little look around...
TalkingToPolice: ^What is this? You told me that the officer had to take my statement.
^Detective, I said that you were supposed to take his statement.
^Oh. Sorry! Let me do that now real quick.
^You already took my statement! Fuck this. Don't waste my time again.
^You don't know the half of it.
InquireAboutReceipt: ^I have proof that he came here. I have a receipt from this shop that matches his card number.
^Did you know he constantly lost his wallet? Someone must have found it and treated themself to one of my famous rolls. It's not the first time it's happened, or the last.
^Though, I guess this actually would be the last time...
^I guess that makes sense... The officer told me he would lose it a lot. Even the bartender said so.
^To weasel out of paying his tab, no doubt!
^Look, he didn't come here, alright? But I guess I could keep an eye out in case it shows up again.
DeadEnd: ^He knows more than he lets on. Call it a hunch, but I've seen this type of guy before...
^Som på Bollen i Rullen 25 på Nobeltorget.
^Maybe he really doesn't know anything. Or I could take a little look around his shop... But I need to find a way to distract him somehow.

g-0: ^Här tar det stopp! Går tillbaka till hubben

Relation: ^Sure, if being pestered by an alcoholic in nearly every aspect of my life counts as knowing him.
^Alright, he wasn't all bad. He was a paying customer, which isn't something that most people would agree with me on.
^He goes to the bar pretty much every day, drinking his life away. He's racked up a pretty sizeable tab I've heard, which won't ever get paid off at this point.
^The bartender is too good on him. Peter knew that shit wouldn't fly with me though, but the bartender needs all the business he can get. Even if that business is putting him deeper in debt.
^I see. I'll make sure to talk to him as well, but right now I'm more interested in your relationship to the victim.
^Sorry, I can't tell you anything that I haven't already said.
Wallet: ^Did you steal that from my shop?
^It's not stealing if it's not yours, is it?

^I don't like what you're implying. Look, I know it belonged to Peter. He would lose his wallet constantly. I find it, hold on to it, he comes back the next day and asks if I found it. Rinse and repeat.
^He would just expect me to have it, and we would keep having this little back and forth.
^Only this time he never came back...
^When did you find this wallet, exactly?
^Let's see... I must have found it sometime before 2 A.M. He probably dropped it after he left the bar.
^And you're sure that he never came here at any point last night?
^Like I said. Are we done here?

b: ^He's only telling half the truth. I can't press this issue further though, I have to find something more tangible and come back to this.

WalletCont: ^The receipts you found in the trash earlier contradict this statement. They clearly show that a purchase was made with his card after the time Boss Man supposedly found the wallet.
^That doesn't add up. Take a look at this receipt here.
^You see Boss Man's eyes glance across the receipt, while he's trying to understand where you're going with this.
^I'd recognize a receipt from my shop, obviously. What's the angle here?
^Well, guess who this receipt belonged to?
^His eyes stop and dart up from the receipt to look back at you.
^That's right bitch boy... It's clear as day that Peter was here last night, after 2 A.M and shortly before his estimated time of death, buying his nightly falafel.
^Ok, wait a minute here, hold on... I just forgot he came here. It was a busy night, and I was tired, yeah?
^Bullshit artist. Even if that were true, that would mean he still had his wallet on him. And now it's here, with you instead... See where I'm going with this?
^He came here last night, bought a falafel, and as he walked away, you went after. Killed him, took his wallet. Bam.
^No, you're wrong! I didn't kill the poor guy! Let me explain...
^What's there to explain? You have his wallet, the only way you could have it is if you took it from his body.
^Like you took his wallet, I'm taking you... in, for murder.
^No, wait! I DID take his wallet after I found his body, yeah? But I didn't KILL HIM!
LastNight: ^It's true, I found his body and decided to take his wallet. I was just gonna grab some cash and then give it back to him like normal the next day.
^Just like I told you before, I soon realized that he was actually dead and so I called the cops.
^Then I realized that I still had his wallet, which wasn't a good look for me. I couldn't admit something like that, that would make me look guilty! So I never mentioned it.
^He died shortly after leaving your shop. You know how it looks, right?
^I didn't do it, please believe me!
^Why should I? You lied to me before, you might still be lying.
^You could have knocked him the fuck out, and it happened to kill him. Let's just say it was manslaughter and call it a day, huh? Heat of the moment!
^I overheard you speaking with the Officer earlier, and he clearly stated that the victim couldn't have died from that! That means I couldn't have done it, yeah?

b: ^It's very likely that the victim was poisoned, and this falafelrulle right here could be the culprit.
^That's bullshit, could means that you don't have any real proof!
^No, I mean eat it. This falafelrulle. If it's not poisoned, it should be fine to eat, right?
^I'm... I'm not eating that. I don't know how old that thing is, and you could have put something in it yourself!
^And I no longer have any reason to trust you. I have everything I need here.
^Wait! I have information! I can tell you about something Peter told me last night.

c-1: ^Not interested. Like I said, I have everything I need here.

b: ^I guess that makes sense. I don't think I have anything to prove an alternative theory.
^Still, I know I'm onto something here... I'm just missing a small piece. I could ask around and see if I find out something interesting.

StoreClerkFight: ^When he came here last night and bought his nightly falafelrulle, he told me that he and the cashier down by the gas station had an argument about something.
^So what? That doesn't mean anything. I can imagine that a lot of people had a bone to pick with Peter.
^Yeah, yeah; but this was something else. He would always tell me whenever he got into trouble with the police, or if he was kicked out of the bar because of his shitty karaoke, and so on.

^But last night... He seemed off. When I asked him about what he and the cashier were arguing about, he got pissed and started yelling at me. So I didn't press him any further, and then he left. And you know the rest.
^I could choose to follow up on this lead, and even if it leads nowhere; I still have my prime suspect right here.
^Alright. I'll see where this goes, but don't expect to be in the clear because of this.
UnlockSuspect: ^You have unlocked Boss Man as a suspect. You can now speak with The Officer regarding this or consult your Field Manual for further information.
Call: ^Didn't you already take my statement about this?
^The officer did, yes. I would like to hear it as well. Spare me no details, capische?
^Last night, at maybe around 2 A.M, I closed up shop and headed home.
^That's when I stumbled upon him. I though for sure that he had just passed out from another night of drinking.
^But... he wasn't breathing. I tried to wake him up, but I got no response.
^I realized he wasn't just black out drunk, and quickly called the cops. They arrived almost immediately, I didn't even have time to...
^...you know, do anything else. I mean, I didn't know what to do really.
^He didn't come by here last night then? I was told he was a regular here, to a fault almost.
^No, he didn't. That's all I have to say about that.

murder_scene: ^The bedroom. This is where it happened. Now to look for clues.
^Use the cane to reach under the bed

c-0: ^The bed was low to the ground, but not so low something might not roll underneath. It was still neatly made.
^I lifted back the bedcover. The duvet underneath was crumpled.
c-1: ^Careful not to disturb anything beneath, I removed the cover entirely. The duvet below was rumpled.
^Not the work of the maid, who was conscientious to a point. Clearly this had been thrown on in a hurry.
c-2: ^I pulled back the duvet. Beneath it was a sheet, sticky with blood.
^Either the body had been moved here before being dragged to the floor - or this is was where the murder had taken place.
c-3: ^Carefully, I pulled the bedsheets back into place, trying to make it seem undisturbed.
c-4: ^I pushed the bed with spread fingers. It creaked a little, but not so much as to be obnoxious.
c-5: ^Lying down, I peered under the bed, but could make nothing out.
c-6: ^I took a step back from the bed and looked around.

c-1: ^I peered under the bed. Something glinted back at me.

c-0: ^I fished with one arm under the bed, but whatever it was, it had been kicked far enough back that I couldn't get my fingers on it.
c-2: ^I stood up once more, and brushed my coat down.
^Positioning the cane above the carpet, I gave the glinting thing a sharp tap. It slid out from the under the foot of the bed.

c-0: ^Satisfied, I stood up, and saw I had knocked free a bloodied knife.
c-1: ^Moving the cane aside, I looked under the bed once more, but there was nothing more there.

c-3: ^Careful not to touch the handle, I lifted the blade from the carpet.
c-4: ^The blood was dry enough. Dry enough to show up partial prints on the hilt!
c-5: ^I turned my attention to the desk. A lamp sat in one corner, a neat, empty in-tray in the other. There was nothing else out.
^Leaning against the desk was a wooden cane.

c-0: ^I picked up the wooden cane. It was heavy, and unmarked.
c-2: ^I regarded the in-tray, but there was nothing to be seen. Either the victim's papers were taken, or his line of work had seriously dried up. Or the in-tray was all for show.
c-4: ^I took a step away from the desk once more.

c-6: ^I was still holding the cane: I gave it an experimental swoosh. It was heavy indeed, though not heavy enough to be used as a bludgeon.
^But it might have been useful in self-defence. Why hadn't the victim reached for it? Knocked it over?
c-7: ^I went over to the window and peered out. A dismal view of the little brook that ran down beside the house.

b: ^Through the steamed glass I couldn't see the brook.

c-0: ^I watched the little stream rush past for a while. The house probably had damp but otherwise, it told me nothing.
c-1: ^The glass in the window was greasy. No one had cleaned it in a while, inside or out.
c-2: ^A cold day outside. Natural my breath should steam.
c-3: ^I breathed gently on the glass once more.

b: ^I looked away from the dreary glass.
^ The steam from my breath faded.

c-4: ^I leant back from the glass. My breath had steamed up the pane a little.

b: ^ A little light spilled under the bed.
^ The light gleamed on the polished tabletop.

compare_prints: ^Compare the prints on the knife and the window

c-0: ^Holding the bloodied knife near the window, I breathed to bring out the prints once more, and compared them as best I could.
^Hardly scientific, but they seemed very similar - very similiar indeed.

s0: ^But I could see a few fingerprints, as though someone hadpressed their palm against it.
s1: ^The fingerprints were quite clear and well-formed.

seen_light: ^ Move the light to the bed
^ Move the light back to the desk

c-1: ^I moved the light over to the bloodstain and peered closely at it. It had soaked deeply into the fibres of the cotton sheet.
^There was no doubt about it. This was where the blow had been struck.
c-2: ^I moved the light back to the desk, setting it down where it had originally been.
c-3: ^I picked the light up and set it down on the floor.

joe_in_hall: ^My police contact, Joe, was waiting in the hall. 'So?' he demanded. 'Did you find anything interesting?'

s: ^'They match a set of prints on the window, too.'
^'The body was moved to the bed at some point
^'The victim was murdered in bed, and then the body was moved to the floor.'

c-1: ^'Good going!' Joe replied with a grin. 'We thought the murderer had gotten rid of it. I'll bag that for you now.'
c-2: ^'Hrm. Not very complete. It'll be hard to get a match from these.'
c-3: ^'Anyone could have touched the window,' Joe replied thoughtfully. 'But if they're more complete, they should help us get a decent match!'
c-4: ^,' I told him. 'And then moved back to the floor.'

s: ^'Perhaps to get something from the floor?'

c-1: ^'You wouldn't move a whole body for that.'
c-2: ^'It's just speculation at this point,' Joe remarks.

s: ^'Perhaps the murderer wanted to mislead us.'
^'Perhaps the murderer hoped to clean up the scene.'
^'They wanted us to think the victim was awake
^'They wanted us to think there was some kind of struggle

c-0: ^, I replied thoughtfully. 'That they were meeting their attacker, rather than being stabbed in their sleep.'
c-1: ^,' I replied. 'That the victim wasn't simply stabbed in their sleep.'
g-0: ^'But if they were killed in bed, that's most likely what happened. Stabbed, while sleeping.'

c-6: ^'All right. It's a start,' Joe replied.

b: ^ 'I'll get those prints from the window now.'
^ 'I'll run those prints as best I can.'
